3 They have taken crafty counsel against thy. people, and consulted against thy hidden. ones.

“But if all have free agency in the Book of Mormon, all do not have civil liberties. The Zoramites, as we have seen, had a thought-police, ‘therefore they found out privily the minds of all the people,’ and forthwith deported ‘those who were in favor of the words . . . spoken by Alma’” (Hugh Nibley, Since

Was not arrayed (oude periebaleto). Middle voice and so "did not clothe himself," "did not put around himself." (Robertson's NT Word Pictures)

 hearts of the people to stir them up to anger against you, that
 they will not believe my words.

Zoramites became Lamanites Frequently in the Book of Mormon, Lamanites is a general term that includes those who oppose the Nephites.
Zoramites became Lamanites Frequently in the Book of Mormon, Lamanites is a general term that includes those who oppose the Nephites.

committed many murders (Alma 24:11). They refused to "take up arms against their brethren; nay, they would not even make any preparations for war (Alma 24:5). They even buried their weapons to preserve their covenants. An interesting conclusion to the Anti-Nephi-Lehies was the change of their name when they are finally integrated into the Nephite society and are called the people of Ammon (Alma 27:26). (Lee Donaldson, GEMS)

committed many murders (Alma 24:11). They refused to "take up arms against their brethren; nay, they would not even make any preparations for war (Alma 24:5). They even buried their weapons to preserve their covenants. An interesting conclusion to the Anti-Nephi-Lehies was the change of their name when they are finally integrated into the Nephite society and are called the people of Ammon (Alma 27:26). “Corianton's sins were grievous. And yet we have every reason to believe that Alma's preaching touched the soul of his errant son, that Corianton ‘crossed himself’ (see Alma 39:9), repented, and returned to the ministry. We read of Corianton's labors a year or so later: ‘Thus ended the nineteenth year of the reign of the judges over the people of Nephi. Yea, and there was continual peace among them, and exceedingly great prosperity in the church because of their heed and diligence which they…

33-35 Moroni made plans to attack the City Morianton. The Lamanites, notwithstanding their losses in other fields of warfare, were continually strengthening the City Morianton, which was on the east seashore. New supplies backed by added forces from other fields of conflict were making the city an exceedingly strong refuge into which the Lamanites might gather. Whether or not Moroni, despite his plans, attacked the city, we do not know. The Sacred Record makes no mention of it, and surmise is…

Il. 1. the twenty-second day of the first month evidently ended the 120 days’ rest, so that the return to ‘ Nineveh’ took place just before the end of the ninth month of the seventeenth-year of Neb. The precise date is meant to give the appearance of real history.
CHAPTER XVI. Ahaz wicked reign—He spotleth the templ — Hezekiah succeedeth him. :; N the seventeenth. year of Pekat the son of Remaliah, Abaz ‘the son of Jotham king of Judah began to. reign.

Il. 1. the twenty-second day of the first month evidently ended the 120 days’ rest, so that the return to ‘ Nineveh’ took place just before the end of the ninth month of the seventeenth-year of Neb. The precise date is meant to give the appearance of real history.

Thus commenced the reign of the judges] After Lehi left Jerusalem, time was reckoned according to the date of his departure-e.g., "fifty years after Lehi left Jerusalem," or "four hundred years had passed away from the time that Lehi left Jerusalem," and so forth. With the establishment of the system of the judges we encounter a new reckoning of time among the Nephites-the "first year of the reign of the judges," the "fifteenth year of the reign of the judges," and so on. This system would be…
CHAPTER XVI. Ahaz wicked reign—He spotleth the templ — Hezekiah succeedeth him. :; N the seventeenth. year of Pekat the son of Remaliah, Abaz ‘the son of Jotham king of Judah began to. reign.

“At the beginning of their history the Nephites put up a mobile defense against their enemies, making skillful use of the wilderness to "fortify against them with their armies" (Jacob 7:25). This method was never given up, as we can see in the ordering of the evacuation of the land of Jershon which "gave place in the land . . . for the armies of the Nephites, that they might contend with the armies of the Lamanites" (Alma 35:13). But in the third generation the Nephites "began to fortify our…

" ... the people began to wax hard, and ... they began to be offended because of the strictness of the word." (Alma 35:15) "Strict" comes from the root that means "narrow, drawn in, small, to squeeze (hence powerful), furrow or groove. It has the sense of being rigorous and stringent in laws or rules and is characterized as a close and unrelaxing effort" (Chambers Dictionary of Etymology). The definition of strict is exact, not vague or loose, enumerated or described in exact detail; stringent…
his heart was exceedingly sorrowful because of the separation of the Zoramites from the Nephites.

“In the beginning of marriage and family life, Lucy [Mack Smith] had led the way for the Smith family’s search for religion, studying the Bible and discussing their findings. Many years later, when Father Smith lay dying, the tender family circle about him showed the literal pattern of their lifestyle according to the Word. Just before he died Father Smith followed the exemplary pattern of bestowing a father’s blessing upon each child individually and having it recorded as did Jacob of old, and…
